HospitalRecommendations.Name: Fortis Escorts Heart Institute
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: A globally renowned hospital for Cardiac Sciences, specializing in complex cardiac procedures like CABG. They have a dedicated team for international patients to assist with all logistical needs.

HospitalRecommendations.Name: Apollo Hospitals, Greams Road
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: A pioneer in private healthcare in India with a flagship Centre of Excellence for Cardiology and Cardiothoracic Surgery. Chennai is a major medical hub with excellent infrastructure.

HospitalRecommendations.Name: Narayana Institute of Cardiac Sciences
HospitalRecommendations.Specialization: Part of the Narayana Health City, this hospital is famous for performing one of the largest numbers of heart surgeries in the world at an affordable cost, maintaining high-quality outcomes.

Itinerary.Notes: Arrive at Indira Gandhi International Airport (DEL). A representative from the chosen hospital (e.g., Fortis) can arrange a comfortable airport transfer to a pre-booked hotel or serviced apartment near the hospital. Settle in and rest.

Visit Fortis Escorts for your initial consultation with the cardiac surgeon and cardiologist. Undergo all necessary pre-operative investigations like blood tests, ECG, Echocardiogram, and Chest X-ray. The international patient coordinator will guide you.
Get admitted to the hospital. The medical team will review your test results, manage your current medications (aspirin may be stopped temporarily), and prepare you for surgery.
Undergo Coronary Artery Bypass Surgery. Your family will be updated by the surgical team and can wait in the designated area.
Post-operative recovery in the Cardiac Care Unit (CCU) under constant monitoring.

Transfer to a private room. Focus on recovery with the help of physiotherapists for breathing exercises and gentle mobilization. A dietician will provide a customized cardiac and diabetic-friendly meal plan.
After a final check-up, you will be discharged. Settle back into your serviced apartment. The hospital will provide detailed discharge instructions and medications.

Rest is paramount. Gentle, short walks of 5-10 minutes are encouraged. During this week, you will have a follow-up appointment with your surgeon to check on your healing. Your son and daughter-in-law can explore the local area.

Itinerary.Activity: Receive Medical Clearance & Begin Gentle Sightseeing (Delhi)
Itinerary.Notes: After final clearance from your doctor for light travel, begin your sightseeing. Visit Lodhi Garden for a peaceful walk and Humayun's Tomb (wheelchair accessible). Hire a private car for all travel.

Visit the serene Akshardham Temple (utilize their cart/wheelchair service) for a spiritual experience. Later, take a comfortable drive through Lutyens' Delhi to see India Gate and Rashtrapati Bhavan (President's House).
Travel to Agra (approx. 3-4 hours) in a comfortable, pre-booked car. Check into your hotel and rest for the remainder of the day.
Visit the Taj Mahal at sunrise. It is a flat walk, and battery-operated carts and wheelchairs are available to take you to the entrance. Avoid crowds and heat. Rest in the afternoon.
Travel to Jaipur, the 'Pink City' (approx. 4-5 hours). En route, you can visit Fatehpur Sikri if you feel up to it (mostly a driving view). Check into your hotel and relax.
Visit the City Palace and Jantar Mantar (astronomical observatory). Both are largely on flat ground. Enjoy a view of the Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds) from the road.
Visit Amer Fort. You can take a car/jeep right up to the top to avoid climbing. Explore the fort at a very slow pace. In the evening, enjoy a cultural dinner at a resort like Chokhi Dhani.

Based on your interest in spiritual healing, travel to a calmer location like Pushkar (near Jaipur) or take a short flight from Delhi to Dehradun to visit Rishikesh. Engage in gentle activities like attending evening 'aarti' ceremonies, light meditation, or simply relaxing by the holy rivers. This is a flexible period for rest and rejuvenation.
Travel back to Delhi and rest.
Have a final consultation with your surgeon in Delhi. Get all your medical reports and a 'fit to fly' certificate. Do some last-minute souvenir shopping if you feel energetic.
Fly back to Australia from Delhi.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: A beautiful, serene park with historical tombs. The flat, well-paved pathways are perfect for the short, gentle walks recommended post-surgery. It's peaceful and not overly crowded on weekdays.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: Located near Khan Market, which has many upscale cafes and restaurants (e.g., 'The Big Chill Cakery', 'Perch Wine & Coffee Bar') that can cater to specific dietary requests like low-salt and low-sugar meals.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: A magnificent UNESCO World Heritage site with stunning Mughal architecture. The grounds are expansive and flat, and the main structure can be admired with minimal walking. It offers a great cultural experience without being physically strenuous.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: A short drive from Nizamuddin East and Sunder Nursery, which has a wonderful lakeside cafe ('Fabcafe by the Lake') known for its healthy and organic Indian cuisine.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: Fulfills the desire for a spiritual experience. The complex is vast but has excellent accessibility, including free electric cart and wheelchair services. The exhibitions and boat ride are seated activities, making it very comfortable for someone recovering.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: The temple has a large vegetarian food court ('Premvati Food Court') that offers simple, clean, and sattvic (pure) food, which aligns well with a healthy recovery diet.

RecoveryAndSightseeing.WhyRecommended: For days when even light walking is too much, a comfortable car ride through the heart of New Delhi is ideal. You can see major landmarks like India Gate, the Parliament House, and the President's Estate (Rashtrapati Bhavan) from the car, offering a great sightseeing experience with zero physical effort.
RecoveryAndSightseeing.NearbyDietaryOptions: The route is close to Connaught Place, which has a vast array of restaurants, including 5-star hotels like The Imperial or The Claridges, whose chefs are well-equipped to handle any dietary restriction.

DietaryNeedsAndAccommodation.HospitalMeals: Yes, all recommended hospitals have a dedicated nutrition and dietetics department. A dietician will consult with you and your doctor to create a personalized meal plan (e.g., a therapeutic, low-sodium, diabetic-friendly cardiac diet) that also considers your personal taste preferences.
DietaryNeedsAndAccommodation.NearbyAccommodations: It is highly recommended to stay in a serviced apartment for the recovery period. This provides access to a private kitchen, allowing your family to prepare meals that strictly adhere to your dietary needs. Major hotel chains near the hospitals also offer in-room dining and have chefs who can accommodate special requests for healthy, customized meals.
